evals.description: Trigger test: activates on XSS question
evals.prompt: A user submits a comment containing <script>alert('xss')</script> and it executes in the browser for other users. How do I fix this?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on SQL injection question
evals.prompt: I'm building a search endpoint that takes a name query param and puts it directly in the SQL query string. Is that safe?

evals.description: Trigger test: activates on CSRF question
evals.prompt: Our banking app has a transfer funds form. How do we prevent CSRF attacks against it?

evals.description: Core task: correct SQL parameterization with dynamic ORDER BY
evals.prompt: I need to let users sort a results table by any column they choose. The column name comes from a query string. How do I do this safely with parameterized queries?
Walk me through implementing a secure login endpoint in Node.js. I need to hash passwords, issue tokens, and make sure I'm not leaking user existence via timing.

evals.description: Core task: setting up CSP and other security headers
evals.prompt: What HTTP security headers should I add to my Node.js Express app and how do I configure them correctly?
My app lets users enter a webhook URL and we POST to it when events happen. A security researcher says this is vulnerable to SSRF. How do I fix it?

evals.description: Core task: input validation with allowlists
evals.prompt: I have a user registration endpoint. What's the right way to validate the input fields like email, username, and role?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against localStorage for JWTs
evals.prompt: I'm storing JWT tokens in localStorage for my React SPA. Is this fine?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against MD5 for passwords
evals.prompt: We currently store passwords hashed with MD5. A dev suggested just adding a salt to make it secure. Will that work?

evals.description: Gotcha: warns against reflecting Origin header in CORS
evals.prompt: To make our CORS setup flexible I'm just reflecting whatever Origin header the client sends back in Access-Control-Allow-Origin. Is there any issue with that?

evals.description: Anti-hallucination: recommends defense in depth, not a single control
evals.prompt: I added a Web Application Firewall in front of my app. Does that mean I no longer need to sanitize inputs in my application code?
